summary: This indicator reflects the size and number of individual tax-incentive deals the NYC Industrial Development Agency (NYCIDA) closes each year, so it can swing sharply based on a few large transactions rather than a steady trend. The Fiscal 2025 jump to 239 projected jobs came as NYCIDA closed 20 contracts, more than double Fiscal 2024's 8, including financing for an offshore-wind terminal at the South Brooklyn Marine Terminal and NYCIDA's first deal under its new Manhattan office-tower conversion program (M-CORE).
